graded_findings.rationale: The gather found this test updated appropriately for the core sampler behavior, including rate handling, sampling decisions, fatal bypass, disabled behavior, and concurrency coverage. There is no adherence gap to act on for this finding.

The gather found this test updated appropriately for configuration update paths, invalid retention, disabled and full-rate folding, boundaries, and request-only derivation. This is not a merge-blocking concern.

The gather found this integration test updated appropriately for macro behavior, payload suppression, diagnostic sampling, bypass paths, and VLOG/background behavior. No substantive test gap is identified here.

The gather found this test updated appropriately for access sampling, force-retention, bypass behavior, per-event sampling, type mapping, and formatting. There is no actionable deficiency in this item.

The gather found this test updated appropriately for the new typed AccessRecorder facades, deferred providers, skipped providers, recorder variants, and key lifetime behavior. This finding does not indicate a gap.
The gather found this test updated appropriately for MetaPb log_sample_state propagation across disabled, full-rate, zero-rate, random, and undecided receiver cases. No blocking issue is present.

The gather found this system test updated appropriately for worker-to-client sampler config delivery and cross-process request sampling through KV operations. This is adequate coverage rather than an issue.

The gather found this test updated appropriately with hot-path performance guardrails for request sampling, payload suppression, access sampling, and sampler-only cost. The item does not identify an adherence problem.
The gather found this test updated appropriately for the shift from LogRateLimiter to LogSampler and request stream-expression evaluation. No additional action is warranted from this item.
The gather found this test updated appropriately for sampler-enabled and sampler-disabled access-log marker semantics. This finding is not a real adherence gap.

The gather identifies a touched test file that still misses the new sampler flag dynamic update paths, including batch validation, rollback on invalid rates, and explicit-state behavior. Because these paths affect runtime configuration correctness, the missing coverage is a substantive gap that should stop merge.
